response: The life of this influential art historian was defined by a profound engagement with the cultural rebirth known as the Renaissance, a period she studied with unparalleled rigor. Her early work focused on documenting the masterpieces of the Renaissance, creating a comprehensive catalogue that became the definitive reference for scholars worldwide. As her career progressed, she expanded her research to include the lesser-known artists of the Renaissance, ensuring their contributions were not lost to time. A second major catalogue she compiled revealed new connections between regional styles and the broader movement of the Renaissance. Her meticulous approach to archival research set a new standard for the field, transforming how historians understand the era. Even in her later years, she continued to refine her scholarship, producing a third essential catalogue that remains a cornerstone of modern art historical study. Her legacy in art history is unmatched.
